reasoning: As of early 2025, South Sudan remains one of the few African nations that have yet to ratify the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement. While the number of ratifications continent-wide has increased to 48 by February 2025, extensive searches yield no news of parliamentary motions, debates, or government announcements regarding South Sudan's ratification in 2024 or 2025. South Sudan was consistently ranked lowest in AfCFTA implementation readiness in earlier reports. Given the country's political and institutional fragility, legislative action would typically be preceded by significant public announcements or international pressure, which are entirely absent. With less than two months remaining until the July 1, 2025 deadline, the probability of sudden, unannounced ratification and deposit of instruments is extremely low.
